records.ety: [OE. triklen, probably for striklen, freq. of striken to flow, AS. strīcan. See Strike, v. t.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Trickled (trk"k'ld); p. pr. & vb. n. Trickling (trk"klng).]

defs: To flow in a small, gentle stream; to run in drops.
quotes: His salt tears trickled down as rain.
Fast beside there trickled softly down A gentle stream.

defs: The act or state of trickling; also, that which trickles; a small stream; drip.
quotes: Streams that . . . are short and rapid torrents after a storm, but at other times dwindle to feeble trickles of mud.
